{{Quote|You're no ''match'' for me! ''No one'' is!|Mystra, to Han Solo|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
'''Mystra''' was an [[Alien/Legends|alien]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] who served as an enforcer for [[Raskar]], a former [[Iridium Pirates/Legends|pirate]] and the ruler of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Antipose]]. Raskar organized profitable [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] competitions with a rare [[power gem]] as their ultimate prize, and Mystra helped him maintain order on the planet, which included [[Death/Legends|killing]] undesirable individuals on her employer's behalf.

In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], when the [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] arrived on Antipose in search of a power [[Gemstone/Legends|gem]], Mystra interrupted a confrontation they had found themselves in with a [[Unidentified Human 1 (Antipose)|trio]] [[Unidentified Human 2 (Antipose)|of]] [[Unidentified alien (Antipose)|competitors]] for the artifact. The enforcer took the pair to meet Raskar, who demonstrated the power gem to Solo and attempted to persuade the smuggler to participate in the [[Antipose Arena|arena]] fighting for it. Suspecting that Solo would attempt to find a way to obtain the gem outside the system, Mystra then tried to intimidate the smuggler with her skill with a [[Wrist blaster/Legends|wrist blaster]].

Nevertheless, Solo eventually had Chewbacca start an impromptu brawl, observed by Mystra from afar, with the [[Unidentified gladiator|leading gladiator]] in order to create a distraction that allowed him to sneak into [[Raskar's villa]] and perform a close [[Portable scanner/Legends|scan]] of the power gem. Determining that the artifact's useful properties had diminished over [[Time/Legends|time]]—a fact that Raskar had not disclosed to any of the contestants competing for it—Solo ultimately convinced the ex-[[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] to let him and Chewbacca leave with the gem, and Raskar and Mystra both saw the smugglers off as they departed the planet.

==Biography==
===Enforcer for a planetary ruler===
{{Quote|These days, ''Mystra'' does all my killing!|Raskar|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
[[File:Raskar.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Mystra worked as a bodyguard and enforcer for Raskar.]]
Mystra was a [[Gunslinger/Legends|gunslinger]] and [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]]<ref name="Gladiators">{{SW|url=news/galactic-gladiators-great-warriors-of-star-wars|text=Galactic Gladiators: Great Warriors of ''Star Wars''|int=Galactic Gladiators: Great Warriors of Star Wars|nolive=1}}</ref> who served as a [[Bodyguard/Legends|bodyguard]]<ref name="PowerGem">"[[The Power Gem]]"</ref> and enforcer for [[Raskar]],<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> the ruler of the<ref name="ANewBeginning">"[[A New Beginning]]"</ref> [[Centrality/Legends|Centrality]]<ref name="TEA Antipose">''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Antipose system/Legends|Antipose system]]</ref> [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> [[Antipose]].<ref name="UnstatedCanon">{{Blog|danwallace|153|Unstated Canon in Star Wars: The Essential Atlas}}</ref> A former [[Iridium Pirates/Legends|pirate]], Raskar had [[Centran|settled]] on Antipose with a valuable [[power gem]]<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />—one of several rare artifacts composed of a<ref name="CSWE power">{{CSWECite|III|40|power gem}}</ref> [[Meteorite/Legends|meteoritic]]<ref name="DoomMission">"[[Doom Mission]]"</ref> [[Mineral/Legends|mineral]] that could disable the [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]] of [[Starship/Legends|starships]]<ref name="CSWE power" />—in his possession. He then organized a system of [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiatorial]] competitions with the power [[Gemstone/Legends|gem]] as the prize for the ultimate winner.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

Over [[Time/Legends|time]], many individuals sought to participate in the gladiatorial fights, with Raskar collecting [[Antipose Arena|arena]] admissions—from both the audience and the participants—as well as percentages from all associated [[Gambling/Legends|betting]]. Mystra helped the former [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] maintain order on Antipose, [[Death/Legends|killing]] any undesirable individuals on his behalf. Raskar, however, never made it known to Mystra that, in addition to a [[Force field/Legends|force field]], the power gem, kept in a chamber at [[Raskar's villa|his villa]] in the planet's [[Antipose city|only city]], was also protected by concealed destructive energy beams.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

===Meeting Solo and Chewbacca===
{{Quote|You're here for the ''power gem'', Captain Solo… don't you want to meet the man who's giving it ''away''?|Mystra, to Han Solo|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="0 ABY">"[[The Power Gem]]" is set after the events of {{Film|IV}} and before the [[Evacuation of Yavin 4]], and it therefore takes place in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] was tasked by [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] with finding and obtaining a power gem in order to help the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] combat the new<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] of the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']].<ref name="SSDDatabank">{{DB|starship|superstardestroyer|Super Star Destroyer}}</ref> Solo and his [[Pilot/Legends|copilot]], [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]], then traveled in their [[YT-1300 light freighter/Legends|light freighter]], the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], to the<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> [[Tharin sector/Legends|Tharin sector]]<ref name="EAOC Junkfort">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Junkfort system]]</ref> [[Shadowport/Legends|shadowport]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Junkfort Station/Legends|Junkfort Station]] to begin the search. There, an [[Raskar's agent|alien]]—who, unbeknownst to Solo and Chewbacca, was an agent for Raskar and Mystra—pointed the pair to Antipose, claiming he had seen a power gem there. After the two smugglers departed from Junkfort Station, the agent notified Raskar and Mystra of the pair's impending arrival.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

[[File:MystraShootsAtPowerGem-ThePowerGem.jpg|thumb|left|350px|Mystra shooting at the power gem as part of a demonstration to Han Solo.]]
Setting their [[Light freighter/Legends|ship]] down at a landing field outside Antipose's [[City/Legends|city]], Solo and Chewbacca were soon confronted by a [[Unidentified Human 1 (Antipose)|trio]] [[Unidentified Human 2 (Antipose)|of]] [[Unidentified alien (Antipose)|individuals]] who, interested in acquiring the power gem themselves, were attempting to intimidate any potential rivals into leaving the planet. Before any shots could be [[Lightfight|fired]], Mystra arrived at the site on a [[Airspeeder/Legends|floater]], interrupting the confrontation and ordering that the trio harassing Solo and Chewbacca leave Antipose. Addressing Solo both by his name and his identity as a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]], Mystra then ordered the two smugglers onto the floater, whereupon she took them to the city. There, she briefly showed the pair the Antipose Arena—letting Solo know that people were willing to pay for the opportunity to fight for the power gem—before traveling to Raskar's villa.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

Solo and Chewbacca met Raskar there, and Mystra was present when her employer, wanting to motivate Solo to enter the gladiatorial contest in the arena, arranged for a private demonstration of the power gem's potential. Raskar ordered Mystra to shoot at the artifact—thus startling Solo, who was unaware of the force field protecting the gem—and then demonstrated the artifact's adverse effect on the said force field. Since<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> the [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lord]]<ref name="TEA" /> [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] had placed a high [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] on Solo that Raskar was interested in collecting, he then offered to let the smuggler participate in the gladiatorial fights for the power gem for free. Solo agreed on the condition that his mighty [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiee]] companion be allowed to fight on his behalf instead, with Raskar warning him that he would use Mystra to ensure that Solo did not flee in case Chewbacca was defeated in the arena.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

===A smuggler's distraction===
{{Quote|That shifty ''Corellian'' and his ''Wookiee'' friend have started a ''brawl''… with the leading ''contender'' for the power gem!|Mystra|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
[[File:MystraShootsAtSolo-ThePowerGem.jpg|thumb|right|350px|Mystra attempted to intimidate Han Solo with her skills with a wrist blaster.]]
Suspecting that Solo would attempt to somehow game the system of the gladiatorial contests, Mystra confronted the smuggler and his partner after they had left Raskar's villa. The enforcer demonstrated her skill with her weapons and attempted to intimidate Solo into not trying to cheat. Boarding her floater but remaining in contact with Raskar over [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]], Mystra then proceeded to observe Solo and Chewbacca from afar while Solo obtained directions to the training grounds of a hulking [[Unidentified gladiator|alien gladiator]] who was the leading contender for the power gem. The smuggler had indeed hatched a plan to obtain the power gem without either himself or Chewbacca having to fight in the arena. To that end, Solo then convinced his companion to instigate an impromptu brawl with the gladiator, violating the planetary law prohibiting any fighting outside of the arena.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

Mystra notified Raskar of the situation, with the ex-pirate instructing her to immediately break up the fight. The enforcer attempted to do so but soon judged that it was too late for any such effort given the gathering crowd of spectators, believing they would begin rioting if the authorities interfered with the duel. Intent on punishing Solo for cheating him out of his profits from the fight, Raskar ordered Mystra to search for the smuggler and then traveled from his villa aboard a floater of his own in order to assist her.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

Solo, however, had used the distraction provided by the amassing crowd to slip away unnoticed and sneak back into Raskar's villa. Mystra's employer was notified of the smuggler [[Slicer/Legends|slicing]] his way into the power gem's chamber, and both he and Mystra then sped back to the villa. Raskar, however, was confident the power gem's defenses would deter Solo's attempts, and when the villa's alarm system notified him that the chamber's energy beams had been fired, he assumed that the smuggler had perished and accordingly slowed down his floater, to Mystra's surprise. The enforcer then speculated that Solo's demise would make it easier for Raskar to collect the bounty on him.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

===End of the contests===
{{Quote|Now our little deception will never be ''suspected''… thanks to Chewbacca and you!|Mystra, to Han Solo|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
[[File:RaskarMystraSoloPowerGem-ThePowerGem.jpg|thumb|left|350px|Han Solo (right) convinced Raskar (center) to give the power gem to him after discovering the truth about the artifact's limitations.]]
When the pair arrived at Raskar's villa, however, they discovered that Solo had instead tricked the ex-pirate's [[Unidentified Duelist Elite|training droid]] into the path of the destructive beams. Solo had also used a [[Portable scanner/Legends|portable scanner]] to determine that the potency of the power gem's effect on shields was gradually diminishing and that the artifact was only still good for one proper use against a starship. At first, Raskar ordered Mystra to simply kill Solo in order to prevent that information from becoming public, with the enforcer expressing her confidence that the smuggler could not avoid death at her hands.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

Solo, however, instead convinced Raskar to declare Chewbacca the ultimate winner of the competition since the Rebel Alliance was at that point only interested in using the power gem once anyway, and therefore the two smugglers had no interest in publicly revealing Raskar's deception. Since that plan relied on Chewbacca prevailing in the ongoing duel against the gladiator, Mystra began to despair as she observed via monitors that the Wookiee appeared worn out by the fighting. As she and Solo subsequently traveled via floater to the place of the fight, however, the smuggler explained to Mystra that becoming tired merely enraged Chewbacca, and when he informed his companion that their plan had worked and that he no longer needed to prolong the fight as a distraction, the Wookiee swiftly defeated his opponent.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

Mystra and Raskar later saw Solo and Chewbacca off as the pair departed Antipose with the power gem, with the enforcer expressing their gratitude to the two smugglers for allowing them to save their reputation and her employer remarking to Mystra his admiration for Solo as a [[Scoundrel/Legends|scoundrel]].<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> Raskar later experienced financial difficulties and, as a result, was forced to return to [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] piracy.<ref name="ANewBeginning" />

==Personality and traits==
{{Quote|The galaxy's ''full'' of over-confident scoundrels… they all ''expect'' to win, Solo!|Mystra|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
[[File:Mystra.jpg|thumb|right|300px|Mystra was a mysterious and exotic gunslinger.]]
Mystra was a mysterious and exotic<ref name="Gladiators" /> [[Color/Legends|green]]-skinned [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Alien/Legends|alien]].<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> She viewed gladiatorial combat as an "interesting" and "practical" solution to the problem of having many individuals who competed for the ownership of Raskar's power gem.<ref name="PowerGem" /> Mystra had little respect for the contestants in the arena, believing them to be over-confident "scoundrels" and that [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] was full of such individuals. In addition, she viewed Han Solo as being "shifty" and a "schemer," but she did, however, admit to Raskar that the smuggler possessed a certain charm.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

When warning Solo and Chewbacca not to cheat in order to obtain the power gem, Mysta boasted of never missing a shot with her [[Wrist blaster/Legends|wrist blaster]] and never being outdrawn,<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> with Solo coming to view her as a real danger.<ref name="PowerGem" /> However, Mystra soon perceived that Solo would ignore her warning anyway and continue with his own plan of acquiring the gem.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> Mystra also had a vengeful streak, as evidenced by her suggestion to Raskar to make Solo "pay" for the latter's attempt at subverting the gladiatorial arena system to obtain the power gem.<ref name="PowerGem" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|The Corellian maneuvered so the ''sun'' was in your eyes… and he and the Wookiee have you in a ''crossfire''!|Mystra, to a trio of individuals confronting Han Solo|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
A professional killer,<ref name="Guide3">''[[A Guide to the Star Wars Universe, Third Edition, Revised and Expanded]]''</ref> Mystra was observant enough to notice that, during a confrontation, Han Solo had both positioned himself so that the [[Antipose system star|sun]] would be in his opponents' [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] and had also maneuvered so that he and Chewbacca would have them in a crossfire. Mystra speculated that Solo's opponents would not have survived the resultant engagement.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

==Equipment==
{{Quote|With my ''cyber-vision'', I never miss… and with this ''wrist-blaster'', I'm never outdrawn!|Mystra|<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />}}
Mystra wore a dark red [[Jumpsuit/Legends|jumpsuit]] with yellow accents, including a [[Belt/Legends|belt]] and a pair of [[Gauntlet/Legends|gauntlets]], and yellow [[Boot/Legends|boots]]. She also wore a yellow-and-dark-red<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> [[cybervision helmet]]<ref name="Guide3" /> that obscured her eyes<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> and gave her perfect aim at any target.<ref name="Guide3" /> A pair of lethal [[Limb/Legends|wrist]] [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]], capable of delivering a sting-level blast as well as causing a relatively large explosion, were mounted on Mystra's gauntlets and attached by cables to the red visor of her helmet. In her line of work, Mystra also utilized a yellow-and-green floater that was of a similar model to that used by Raskar, had the driver's controls at its front, and could carry at least two passengers.<ref name="PowerGemEpic" />

==Behind the scenes==
{{Quote|Who knows what evil lurks beneath that nifty head gear?|Context entry for Mystra in the article "ComicScan: Star Wars...On A Daily Basis" by [[Charlie Novinskie]]|<ref name="Galaxy Magazine 2">{{GalaxyCite|2|ComicScan|ComicScan: Star Wars...On A Daily Basis}}</ref>}}
Mystra serves as a secondary antagonist in "[[The Power Gem]]," a [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story written by [[Archie Goodwin]], penciled by [[Al Williamson]], and published between [[July 26]] and [[October 3]], [[1982]]<ref name="PowerGemEpic" /> as part of the daily black-and-white [[Star Wars (comic strip)|''Star Wars'' comic strip]] distributed by the [[Los Angeles Times Syndicate]] to major American and Canadian newspapers from [[1979]] to [[1984]].<ref name="Insider2012">{{InsiderCite|Special Edition 2012|The Empire Strips Back}}</ref> It was not until a decade later that a reprint of "The Power Gem," colored by [[Steve Buccellato]] and included in the<ref name="CSW11">{{StoryCite|series=Classic Star Wars|issue1=11|issue2=12|story=The Power Gem}}</ref> [[August]] [[1993]]<ref name="Marvel CSW11">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53765/classic_star_wars_1992_11|text=Classic Star Wars (1992) #11}}</ref> [[Classic Star Wars 11|eleventh issue]] of the [[Dark Horse Comics]]' ''[[Classic Star Wars]]'' comic-book series, established Mystra to have been a humanoid alien.<ref name="CSW11" />
